overview: Gruul Midrange is a deceptively simple deck that focuses on generating card advantage through optimized 2-for-1 trades. It utilizes versatile split cards and vehicles to execute both aggressive early-game plans and grindy midrange strategies. The deck adapts its posture based on the metagame, leaning into fast mana dorks and haste enablers against control or combo, while relying on value engines and removal against aggressive opponents.
win_conditions: Deploying high-impact finishers like Esika's Chariot or Lovestruck Beast // Heart's Desire to close out games
Executing an aggressive turn 2 plan with Reckless Stormseeker // Storm-Charged Slasher to grant haste and win by turn 4-5
Using Chandra, Awakened Inferno to place emblems on the opponent and end the game in long matches

keep_criteria: Hands containing Llanowar Elves or Elvish Mystic for early acceleration
Hands with early interaction like Obliterating Bolt or Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking mana dorks or early-turn cards when facing aggressive decks
Hands composed entirely of high mana cost cards (3 or more) without early plays

examples.reason: Provides turn 1 mana acceleration and multiple options for turn 2 threats or interaction.

early_game: Accelerate mana with Llanowar Elves or Elvish Mystic
Cast turn 2 threats like Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp or Reckless Stormseeker // Storm-Charged Slasher
Use early removal like Obliterating Bolt to disrupt opponent's board
mid_game: Counter opponent's gameplan with targeted removal and sweepers
Make favorable 2-for-1 trades to maintain card advantage
Deploy vehicles like Esika's Chariot or Skysovereign, Consul Flagship for value and pressure
late_game: Close the game with high-impact creatures or vehicles
Use Chandra, Awakened Inferno for emblem-based win conditions
Maintain board presence and resource advantage without relying on card draw

key_cards.name: Llanowar Elves
key_cards.notes: Essential for enabling turn 2 plays and aggressive postures.

key_cards.name: Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp
key_cards.notes: Can be cast as Stomp for early interaction or Giant to deal damage and create a token.

key_cards.name: Reckless Stormseeker // Storm-Charged Slasher
key_cards.notes: Grants haste to creatures and vehicles, enabling a fast turn 4-5 game plan.

key_cards.name: Esika's Chariot
key_cards.notes: Excellent value engine that returns to the battlefield after board wipes like Supreme Verdict.

key_cards.name: The Akroan War
key_cards.notes: Key card for dealing with large creatures like Old-Growth Troll or Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.

key_cards.name: Embercleave
key_cards.notes: Used post-sideboard to aggressively end games before opponent's board wipes resolve.

sequencing_tips: Wait to equip Embercleave until after the opponent responds to your attack to avoid wasting mana on removal.
Cast Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp as Stomp to remove Bloodtithe Harvester, saving The Akroan War for Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.
Account for Brave the Elements when casting removal spells and deciding which creatures to block with.

matchups.advice: Play fast with three-drops, use Esika's Chariot post-wipe, and side in Embercleave and Chandra, Awakened Inferno.
Interact with elves early, use Obliterating Bolt and The Akroan War on big creatures, and side in Embercleave for aggression.
Seek maximum value with favorable trades, avoid filling the board too much post-side, and use Hazoret the Fervent for indestructible pressure.
Be extremely fast, play Reckless Stormseeker // Storm-Charged Slasher immediately, and use Outland Liberator // Frenzied Trapbreaker on Omniscience.
Control early turns with cheap removal, avoid The Akroan War due to Thalia, and play around Brave the Elements.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Gruul Midrange is a deck that has card advantage as its main strategy, since almost all of its plays are optimized to make 2-for-1 trades.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: end the game with your high-impact cards on the board like Esika's Chariot or the creatures Bonecrusher Giant // Stomp and Lovestruck Beast // Heart's Desire

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: During the attack, we have to be careful when casting Embercleave , to let the opponent respond to our attack first and then equip it.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: excellent card to return to the game after Supreme Verdict or Farewell
excellent removal for the opponent's two main creatures: Old- Growth Troll and Cavalier of Thorns
